Explanation:
The report presents a methodologically sound analysis of chromatographic baseline stability and signal-to-noise ratios. Strengths include clear problem definition, well-structured methodology with three sequential steps, and honest acknowledgment of statistical limitations (p > 0.05). The writing is generally professional and appropriate for technical audiences.

However, several limitations affect the overall quality. The depth of analysis is moderate—while the methodology is rigorous, the exploratory analysis of conductivity-induced drift lacks statistical significance and the authors appropriately note insufficient data density. The visualization (Figure 1) effectively displays SNR distributions but intentionally focuses on only the top three high-variance runs, limiting comprehensive insight into all 11 runs. Originality is modest; the approach combines standard techniques (rolling standard deviations, linear regression, Pearson correlation) without novel methodological contributions. Scientific rigour is adequate but constrained by sample size limitations acknowledged by the authors. Code documentation in the appendix is thorough and reproducible, which is commendable. The report would benefit from additional figures showing all runs, deeper investigation into Q column noise causes, and discussion of alternative approaches to address insufficient statistical power.
